commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: register service worker via SerwistProvider for iOS PWA

The service worker was built but never registered — SerwistProvider
from @serwist/next/react was missing. Also adds manifest id field
for stable iOS 16.4+ PWA identity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: disable navigationPreload for iOS PWA + sync-complete event

- Disable navigationPreload in SW (broke iOS standalone mode + offline)
- Move themeColor from metadata to viewport export (Next.js warning)
- Emit sync-complete event after syncPendingMutations succeeds
- CaeTab listens for sync-complete to refresh data immediately

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add alembic files to backend Dockerfile for railway up deploys

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: video upload webm support + PWA standalone mode preservation

- Add "webm" to CAE_ALLOWED_TYPES (backend rejected MediaRecorder output)
- Add 5min timeout to file upload requests (was 30s global default)
- Replace hard location.href redirects with event-based auth-logout pattern
- Use router.replace instead of router.push for PWA navigation stability
- Add viewport export to root layout for iOS PWA rendering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: final quality gate — 15 issues fixed from comprehensive audit

CRITICAL:
- Stripe webhook: removed supabase client shadowing in switch cases

HIGH:
- Team plan limits: reads from profiles.plan instead of non-existent workspaces.plan
- Integration config: key whitelist + size limit validation
- Encryption: production guard throws if ENCRYPTION_KEY missing
- Settings: API key encryption order fixed (no plaintext in spread)
- Agents: linked_folders items validated (type, length, no traversal)

MEDIUM:
- UUID validation on conversations/messages and chat routes
- Onboarding use cases: French → English keys
- Register: workspace creation failure returns 500
- Settings: LLM provider validated against allowlist

LOW:
- Welcome email: shield → zap emoji
- Documents pagination: 500 → 100

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: offline file uploads with image/video compression (Session 4)

Photos compressed via Canvas API (5MB→300KB), videos capped at 1Mbps
bitrate (15MB→6MB). Files queue in IDB when offline, sync via FormData
through Axios on reconnect.

- compress-image.ts: Canvas API compression (max 1920px, JPEG 80%)
- offline-cae-service.ts: uploadFile, syncFileUpload, storage quota check
- cae-tab.tsx: handleFileUpload uses offlineCaeService (online + offline)
- video-record-dialog.tsx: videoBitsPerSecond 1Mbps cap

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: offline VT form — mutation queue + sync on reconnect (Session 3)

Queue VT field saves in IndexedDB when offline, sync automatically
via Axios (main thread) on reconnect with 30s polling interval.

- mutation-queue.ts: IDB CRUD with 5-retry limit
- sync-status.tsx: compact offline/pending/syncing/synced indicator
- use-sync-status.ts: polling hook for pending mutation count
- layout.tsx: SyncStatus component + online event listener + periodic sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: offline caching infrastructure — IDB schema + online status hook (Session 2)

- db.ts: IndexedDB schema with mutation-queue, offline-files, cae-cache stores
- constants.ts: cache TTL configuration
- use-online-status.ts: reactive hook for navigator.onLine state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add web app manifest, Serwist service worker, install prompt banner,
and configure Next.js build pipeline for PWA generation.

- manifest.json: standalone terrain app with icons
- sw.ts: Serwist service worker with terrain-scoped caching
- install-prompt.tsx: bottom banner with 7-day dismiss cooldown

- Dockerfile.prod: copy public assets to standalone output
- Dependencies: @serwist/next, serwist, idb

concurrent Azure SQL queries that were timing out during Vercel builds.

commit.message:
Keep viewport-fit=cover during orientation change zoom reset

The temporary viewport meta reset was dropping viewport-fit=cover for 500ms,
causing iOS to briefly show safe-area borders in landscape before restoring
edge-to-edge layout. Now includes viewport-fit=cover in the temporary value.

commit.message:
Remap host ports to avoid conflicts with existing weka stack

GPU server already has services on 5432, 6379, 8080, 8081.
Remap cortex ports: postgres→5433, redis→6380, embedder→8082, api→8090.
Internal Docker networking unchanged (containers use service names).

feat: auto-enrich per-rule enabled flag and session toggle

- auto-enrich.json: add "enabled" field per rule (default true); disabled rules are filtered at load time
- agents.py: _load_enrich_rules() skips rules with enabled=false; auto_enrich_context() checks session auto_enrich flag before running any rules
- tools.py: add auto_enrich to _SESSION_KEYS so !config write auto_enrich false/true works
- docs/ADMINISTRATION.md: document enabled field, Runtime controls section with !config commands
- README.md: add auto-enrich bullet in self-learning features; add auto-enrich.json to config files table

commit.message:
Fix language persistence on Vercel: use automation_settings table

Store active languages as JSON in existing automation_settings table
(key='active_languages') instead of a separate target_languages table.
This avoids DDL and works on Vercel's read-only filesystem.

commit.message:
feat: Add /pcarea command for PC game cheats catalog

Create complete PC catalog system starting with Arc Raiders game:
- Added pc_catalog_data.js with Arc Raiders and 3 cheat providers:
  * Fecurity: Hybrid software with aim & visual features
  * Crooked Arm's: External software with advanced features
  * Ancient: External software with full feature set
- Added pc_catalog_controller.js with embed creation functions
- Added /pcarea slash command to browse PC game cheats
- Integrated PC catalog interactions in selectMenus.js
- Updated interactionRouter.js to handle PC catalog buttons

Features:
- System requirements display (OS, processors, graphics, disk, BIOS)
- Dual pricing display (IDR and USD)
- Additional info (StreamProof, game modes, resolutions)
- Multi-level navigation (games → cheats → details)
- Buy Now button linking to purchase channel
- Ephemeral responses for privacy

Total: 1 PC game with 3 cheat providers available

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Messages loaded from the backend have created_at (ISO timestamp) but

historical messages display their timestamp in the chat UI.

Guide agents to help users configure application environment variables

fix: preserve LIST and MAP types from ConvertedType-only group nodes (#477)

## Summary

- Adds a `ConvertedType` fallback in the group-node path of
`openColumns` (`column.go:362-375`) so that `deprecated.Map` and
`deprecated.List` are correctly mapped to `&mapType{}` and `&listType{}`
when `LogicalType` is absent
- This fixes files from older parquet writers that only set
`ConvertedType` (the legacy format) — the leaf-node path
(`schemaElementTypeOf`) already handled this correctly, but the
group-node path did not
- Adds `TestGroupConvertedTypeFallback` with sub-tests for `list`,
`map`, and `map_key_value` converted types

## Test plan

- [x] `go test -run TestGroupConvertedTypeFallback -v .` — new test
passes
- [x] `go test -run TestSchemaRoundTrip -v .` — no regressions
- [x] `go test -run TestOpenFile -v .` — testdata files still load
correctly
- [x] `go test ./...` — full suite passes

Fixes #57

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Add pre-commit hooks and CI validation (#1)

* Add comprehensive pre-commit hooks and CI validation

Pre-commit hooks configuration:
- General hygiene (trailing whitespace, EOL, merge conflicts)
- Security scanning (detect-secrets, gitleaks, AWS credentials)
- HTML linting (HTMLHint)
- CSS linting (Stylelint)
- JavaScript linting (ESLint)
- Code formatting (Prettier)
- Prose/text validation (Vale)
- Commit message validation (Commitizen)

GitHub Actions workflows:
- CI workflow for linting, security, accessibility, and link checking
- Updated deploy workflow with validation gate before deployment

Configuration files:
- .pre-commit-config.yaml: Pre-commit framework config
- .eslintrc.json: JavaScript linting rules
- .stylelintrc.json: CSS linting rules
- .htmlhintrc: HTML linting rules
- .prettierrc: Code formatting config
- .vale.ini: Prose linting with custom vocabulary
- .gitleaks.toml: Secret detection config
- .secrets.baseline: Detect-secrets baseline
- .pa11yci.json: Accessibility testing config
- package.json: NPM dependencies for linters

commit.message:
Fix investigation plan parse failure by stripping JSON comments from LLM output

LLMs sometimes include line comments (//) or block comments (/* */) in
JSON output, causing "Expecting ',' delimiter" parse errors. Add
strip_json_comments() to remove these before parsing, and apply it in
both repair_truncated_json and _parse_plan fallback paths.

commit.message:
feat(workflows): add WorkflowOutcome for partial failure tracking (#30)

* refactor(delete): extract delete workflow into services layer

The delete_user business logic was embedded directly in handlers/delete.rs
alongside HTTP concerns. Moving it to services/delete_user.rs follows
the same pattern as disable_user and invite_user, completing the
extraction of all multi-step workflows from the handlers layer.

- Add services/delete_user.rs with delete_user() taking explicit deps
  (no AppState) and 6 unit tests
- Rename handler to delete_user_handler to avoid collision with the
  imported service function
- Update lib.rs and test_helpers.rs route references
- Add pub mod delete_user to services/mod.rs

* feat(workflows): add WorkflowOutcome for partial failure tracking

Multi-step workflows can now return non-fatal warnings alongside a
successful result. Previously, session revocation failures during
disable were silently swallowed after audit logging — the admin had
no UI visibility without digging through audit logs.

- Add models/workflow.rs with WorkflowOutcome (warnings: Vec<String>)
- Add utils.rs with shared pct_encode (moved from invite handler scope)
- disable_user returns WorkflowOutcome; failed session revocations are
  added as warnings rather than silently dropped
- delete_user returns WorkflowOutcome for consistency
- disable handler redirects with ?warning= if outcome has warnings
- User detail page accepts ?notice and ?warning query params and
  renders them as flash messages
- Add .flash-warning CSS style (amber)

* docs: add group membership reconciliation plan

Adds a detailed planning section to CLAUDE.md covering the
Keycloak group → Matrix room reconciliation feature:
- Policy model (GROUP_MAPPINGS JSON env var)
- New config vars (SYNAPSE_*, RECONCILE_REMOVE_FROM_ROOMS)
- Synapse connector extensions needed
- reconcile_membership workflow design
- Handler and UI plan
- Implementation order
- Open decisions table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(disable): cap warning URL param at 400 chars

Full warning summaries in redirect query params can grow large
when many sessions fail with verbose errors. Truncate at 400
encoded chars and append an ellipsis — full detail is in the
audit log regardless.

Addresses Codex review suggestion on PR #30.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: YouTube extractor with three-tier fallback (v0.2.0)

Three-tier architecture for reliable YouTube downloads:
- Tier 1: kkdai/youtube (pure Go, fast metadata + direct URLs)
- Tier 2: go-ytdlp (subprocess, handles POT/anti-bot measures)
- Tier 3: ox-browser (CF bypass, page fetch + player response parsing)

Core improvements:
- Add LocalPath field to Media (skip download when extractor provides file)
- Add AudioURL field to Media (separate DASH audio streams)
- Add MergeAudioVideo() for DASH video+audio muxing via ffmpeg
- Update Processor to handle LocalPath and DASH merge

9 new tests (38 total), 0 lint issues.
